---
title: 'Homework #8: Q14'
author: "Andrei Dominic Magno"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

A bag contains one red, two blue, three green, and four yellow balls. A sample of three balls is taken without replacement. Let upper B be the number of blue balls and Y the number of yellow balls in the sample.
Simulate the mean and variance of the total number of blue and yellow balls in the sample.
Upload a your code and output here.

```{r}
bag <- c(rep("R", 1),
         rep("B", 2),
         rep("G", 3),
         rep("Y", 4))

n <- 1000000

# Store totals
totals <- numeric(n)

for (i in 1:n) {
  trial <- sample(bag, size = 3, replace = FALSE)
  totals[i] <- sum(trial %in% c("B", "Y"))
}

# Calculate mean and variance
simMean <- mean(totals)
simVar <- var(totals)

cat("Mean:", simMean, "\n")
cat("Variance:", simVar, "\n")
```
